title: Drynaria tricuspis
taxonomy: Drynaria tricuspis is classified under the family Polypodiaceae, which includes various species of ferns. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Furthermore, it is a species of the genus Drynaria, which contains several species of terrestrial ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns known for their diverse range of species and habitats. Drynaria tricuspis is native to the tropical regions of Asia.
uses: Drynaria tricuspis, also known as Chinese tuckahoe or three-angled tuckahoe, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits. The plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. It is also believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to protect against diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. In addition, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and constipation, due to its anti-diarrheal properties. Furthermore, it has been used as a tonic to enhance overall health and well-being.

This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
